sponsors.name: napari
sponsors.full_description: napari is an open-source Python viewer for interactive exploration of large, multi-dimensional scientific images. Widely used in the scientific Python and bioimaging communities, it provides a fast, extensible platform for visualisation, annotation, and analysis. With a strong focus on community and a thriving plugin ecosystem, napari supports both researchers and developers in building reproducible, scalable imaging workflows.

sponsors.name: Quansight
sponsors.full_description: Organizations rely on open source software to innovate, but many struggle to engage sustainably with the communities behind the projects they depend on. Quansight exists to bridge that gap. Our team of software developers, community leaders, technical writers, and designers actively maintain and contribute to more than 40 open source projects, including CPython, NumPy, Jupyter, conda-forge, Polars, and stdlib.js. We partner with engineering, product, and research teams to solve complex technical challenges, accelerate development, reduce risk, and unlock new value across the PyData ecosystem. At the same time, we ensure that this work strengthens the health, resilience, and long-term sustainability of the open source projects and communities our customers rely on. We believe commercial success and community sustainability should reinforce each other — and we help organizations achieve both.

sponsors.name: Ansys
sponsors.full_description: Our Mission: Powering Innovation That Drives Human Advancement - For over 50 years, Ansys (part of Synopsys) software has enabled innovators to push boundaries with the predictive power of simulation. From sustainable transportation to advanced semiconductors, from satellite systems to life-saving medical devices, our software will power the next great leaps in human advancement.

sponsors.name: engicloud.ai
sponsors.full_description: engicloud.ai is a python-based modelling & simulation platform for engineers and scientists. Beyond our library of 20,000+ cross-checked calculators, connect to APIs with managed secrets, use AI assistants to build entire projects or turn a research PDF into an executable calculator. From node-based logic to verifiable simulations across 20 engineering fields. Every step stays inspectable. No black-box output. Built by DCS Computing GmbH, the team behind LIGGGHTS, CFDEM, and Aspherix. Founded in 2012 in Linz, Austria. Free academic accounts at engicloud.ai - python-based collaborative modeling & simulation platform

sponsors.name: appliedAI Institute for Europe
sponsors.full_description:
The appliedAI Institute for Europe aims to strengthen the European AI ecosystem by engaging in research, developing knowledge around AI, providing trusted AI tools, and creating educational as well as interactive formats around high-quality AI content.

As a non-profit subsidiary of the appliedAI Initiative, the Institute was founded in Munich in 2022. The appliedAI Initiative itself is a joint venture of UnternehmerTUM and IPAI. The Institute is managed by Dr. Andreas Liebl and Dr. Frauke Goll.

The appliedAI Institute for Europe focuses on the people in Europe. It pursues the vision of shaping a common AI community and providing high-quality content in the age of AI for the entire society. By promoting trustworthy AI, the Institute accelerates the application of this technology and strengthens trust in AI solutions.

With a focus on research, knowledge development, research and the provision of trusted AI tools, the appliedAI Institute for Europe provides a valuable resource for companies, organizations, and individuals looking to expand their knowledge and skills in AI. Through educational and interaction formats, the Institute enables an intensive exchange of expertise and fosters collaboration between stakeholders from different fields.

The appliedAI Institute for Europe invites companies, organizations, startups, and AI enthusiasts to benefit from the Institute's diverse offerings and resources. The appliedAI Institute for Europe is supported by the KI-Stiftung Heilbronn gGmbH.

For more information, please visit www.appliedai-institute.de

sponsors.name: EuroPython Society
sponsors.full_description: The EuroPython Society (EPS) is a Swedish non-profit organization which organizes EuroPython conferences, supports the Python community in Europe and holds the rights to the EuroPython conference series and trademarks.

sponsors.name: Python Software Verband e.V.
sponsors.full_description:
The Python Software Verband supports and promotes the Python community in the German-speaking region.

The association is a gathering of companies, freelancers, and individuals who use Python and open-source software for commercial or personal purposes and wish to express their appreciation for the open-source community by engaging with it. The Python Software Verband consolidates resources through member contributions and coordinates support measures that would not be feasible for individual members or would require significant effort.

Examples of support measures include conference events, educational activities, and development support. Details about the possible support forms are described in the support program.

sponsors.name: Python Academy
sponsors.full_description: Python Academy is a specialized Python training company. It offers open courses for individuals as well as customized on-site courses for companies and institutes. It covers a wide range of Python topics from introductory to advanced, Python for scientists and engineers, big data, web development, code optimization and many more. In addition to training, it consults companies that migrate to Python and develop high-quality Python software often in combination with training. The special expertise of the academy is scientific and technical software development with Python.

sponsors.name: Pioneers Hub gGmbH
sponsors.full_description: Pioneers Hub gGmbH is a non-profit limited liability company (gemeinnützige GmbH) based in Heidelberg, Germany, dedicated to fostering innovation, knowledge-sharing, and collaboration among technology and research communities.
